individual_exemptions.taxpayer_eligibility: The taxpayer exemption is not available if the taxpayer can be claimed as a dependent on someone else's return.

metadata.source_note: Official three-page tax-year-2026 Form 1-ES instructions. Page 2 publishes individual exemptions and standard-deduction schedules; page 3 publishes gross-tax Schedules A, B, and C for full-year residents and describes proration for nonresidents and part-year residents. This intake preserves the official estimated-tax source and does not certify final-return liability, RuleSpec semantics, or PolicyEngine parity.

standard_deduction_head_of_household.schedule: Wisconsin income not over $20,119: $18,030; over $20,119 but not over $58,827: $18,030 less 22.515% of the amount over $20,120; over $58,827 but not over $136,453: $13,960 less 12% of the amount over $20,120; over $136,453: $0.

standard_deduction_married_filing_jointl.schedule: Wisconsin income not over $29,039: $25,840; over $29,039 but not over $159,690: $25,840 less 19.778% of the amount over $29,040; over $159,690: $0.

standard_deduction_married_filing_separa.schedule: Wisconsin income not over $13,779: $12,280; over $13,779 but not over $75,869: $12,280 less 19.778% of the amount over $13,780; over $75,869: $0.

standard_deduction_single.schedule: Wisconsin income not over $20,119: $13,960; over $20,119 but not over $136,453: $13,960 less 12% of the amount over $20,120; over $136,453: $0.
